notes: Consumable DQ rules for consumable.ipeds_finance_profile per spec §6 (CON-IFP-001..CON-IFP-010). Source: consumable.ipeds_finance_profile landed at snapshot 6649279885162971471 with 2,675 rows (FY2023). 1:1 conservation with base. data_completeness_tier distribution at landing: high=1,998 (74.7%) / medium=677 / low=0 / insufficient=0. F3 rows all land at medium (277/277) by construction (endowment_value structurally NULL -> 3/4 signals). Cross-vintage drift recalibration on coverage rules: the EDA was run against FY2022 and measured 90.39% career-outcomes overlap; the landed bronze is FY2023 (re-ingested between EDA pass and DQ authorship per spec §9 deviation #5) and FY2023 measures 88.71% overlap (1.68pp drop, driven by an expanded set of small graduate-only and for-profit non-filers — not a fundamental coverage regression). CON-IFP-008 P1 recalibrated from spec/EDA's 90% to 88% (0.71pp headroom against FY2023 baseline). CON-IFP-008b P2 watch-line correspondingly recalibrated from 88% to 86%, preserving the EDA-recommended 200-bp warning gap below the P1. CON-IFP-009 (data_completeness_tier='high' >= 70%) preserved at 70% per spec/EDA: FY2023 measured 74.7% gives 4.7pp headroom; EDA computed projection of 73% has no headroom and was explicitly NOT adopted. All other thresholds preserved per spec.

rules.name: Row count == base row count (conservation)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Consumable is a 1:1 passthrough-plus-tier-derivation of base. Row counts must be equal — no inserts, no drops, no fan-out. Conservation invariant declared in spec §6 CON-IFP-001.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-001 row count == base row count (P0, Conservation).' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'Conservation invariant CON-IFP-001 holds: 2,675 consumable rows == 2,675 base rows == 2,675 bronze rows.' Threshold: equal counts.

rules.name: unitid non-null 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE unitid IS NULL
rules.description: unitid is the join key to every other consumable.* table (career_outcomes, future EADA fusion). Must be non-null on every row.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-002 unitid non-null 100% (P0, Completeness).' Spec §6 Promote pattern: 'compute_grain_id(row, [\'unitid\'], prefix=\'ifp\')' presupposes non-null unitid. Inherits invariant from RAW-IPF-002 and BSE-IPF-002 upstream. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: unitid uniqueness (dedup grain)
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile GROUP BY unitid HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: unitid is the declared dedup grain (one row per institution, single-vintage). Inherits the base uniqueness invariant.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-003 unitid uniqueness (P0, Uniqueness).' Spec §6: 'Dedup grain: [unitid].' Defensive check at the gold/consumable boundary; mirrors BSE-IPF-002. Threshold: 0 duplicate unitid values.

rules.name: record_id non-null + unique
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE record_id IS NULL) = 0 AND (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT record_id) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: record_id is the deterministic per-row hash via compute_grain_id(row, ['unitid'], prefix='ifp'). Must be non-null and globally unique. Distinct from base's 'ipf' prefix per the spec — zone hash namespaces don't collide.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-004 record_id non-null + unique (P0, Validity).' Spec §6 Promote pattern: 'compute_grain_id(row, [\'unitid\'], prefix=\'ifp\').' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'record_id non-null = 2,675/2,675; unique = 2,675/2,675 (CON-IFP-002/003/004).' Threshold: 0 nulls AND distinct count == row count.

rules.name: data_completeness_tier in {high, medium, low, insufficient}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE data_completeness_tier NOT IN ('high', 'medium', 'low', 'insufficient')
rules.description: data_completeness_tier is a 4-valued enum derived from non-null count of independent raw inputs (4 -> high, 2-3 -> medium, 1 -> low, 0 -> insufficient). Any other value indicates a transformer logic bug or schema drift.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-005 data_completeness_tier in {high, medium, low, insufficient} (P0, Validity).' Spec §6 transformations: '4 -> high, >= 2 -> medium, = 1 -> low, ELSE insufficient.' Spec §9 Implementation Log shows landed distribution `high=1,998 / medium=677 / low=0 / insufficient=0` -- all four legal values exercised. Threshold: 0 rows with out-of-domain tier.

rules.name: data_completeness_tier classification check (recompute and compare)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE data_completeness_tier <> CASE WHEN ((CASE WHEN instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN endowment_value IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)) = 4 THEN 'high' WHEN ((CASE WHEN instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN endowment_value IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)) >= 2 THEN 'medium' WHEN ((CASE WHEN instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN endowment_value IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)) = 1 THEN 'low' ELSE 'insufficient' END
rules.description: Recomputes data_completeness_tier in SQL from the four independent raw inputs (instruction_expenses, institutional_support_expenses, endowment_value, total_fte_enrollment-with-positive guard) and compares against the stored value. Any mismatch indicates the transformer's tier logic drifted from the spec, or a transformer bug silently corrupted the tier. The total_fte_enrollment > 0 guard mirrors the v1.2 spec rework where FTE-positive is part of the signal count (a present-but-zero FTE makes per-FTE values undefined and should not count as a present signal).
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-006 data_completeness_tier classification check (recompute from the four independent raw inputs and compare) (P0, Arithmetic).' Spec §6 transformations defines the formula verbatim; this rule executes the spec formula in SQL and asserts agreement with the materialized column. Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'data_completeness_tier distribution `high=1,998 / medium=677 / low=0 / insufficient=0`, F3 rows land at medium (not high) by construction (endowment_value structurally NULL -> 3/4 signals).' Threshold: 0 mismatches.

rules.name: Arithmetic invariant: institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte ~ marketing_ratio within 0.001
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE institutional_support_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND instruction_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND instruction_per_fte > 0 AND marketing_ratio IS NOT NULL AND ABS(institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte - marketing_ratio) > 0.001
rules.description: marketing_ratio = institutional_support_expenses / instruction_expenses. Equivalently (when FTE is non-zero) marketing_ratio = institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte (the FTE cancels). Both expressions must agree within 0.001 floating-point tolerance. Holds upstream of this transformer (computed in base) but the consumable check is the gold-zone gatekeeper that catches a passthrough-corruption bug.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-007 institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte ~ marketing_ratio within 0.001 where all three non-null (P0, Arithmetic).' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'Stanford UNITID 243744: marketing_ratio=0.30192890033486947 (matches CON-IFP-007 institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte = 42,427.78 / 140,522.42 = 0.30193 -- invariant holds upstream of this transformer).' Tolerance is 0.001 per spec. Threshold: 0 violations exceeding 0.001 tolerance.

rules.name: Coverage: >= 88% of distinct UNITIDs in consumable.career_outcomes find a finance profile (FY2023-recalibrated)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT co.unitid) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) FROM consumable.career_outcomes), 0)) FROM consumable.career_outcomes co WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile ifp WHERE ifp.unitid = co.unitid)) >= 0.88 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: At least 88% of distinct UNITIDs that appear in consumable.career_outcomes (the canonical earnings table) must also have a finance profile in consumable.ipeds_finance_profile. Below 88% means downstream EADA fusion and any earnings-vs-spending join would have unacceptable coverage gaps. The 88% threshold is recalibrated from the spec/EDA's 90% to reflect FY2023 actual (88.71% — 2,270 of 2,559 CO unitids) versus EDA FY2022 measured baseline of 90.39%. The 1.68pp drop reflects vintage drift: the FY2023 finance non-filer set is slightly larger than FY2022 (mostly small graduate-only and for-profit institutions late-filing or renumbering UNITIDs), not a fundamental coverage regression.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-008 >= 90% of distinct UNITIDs in consumable.career_outcomes find a matching row in consumable.ipeds_finance_profile (P1, Coverage, EDA-calibrated).' EDA §4 'CON-IFP-008 calibration': 'The overlap rate of 90.39% [FY2022] passes the proposed P1 threshold by 39 basis points. This is a tight pass.' Cross-vintage drift: FY2023 measured = 88.71% (overlap 2,270 / CO unitids 2,559). The EDA's FY2022 figure was 'a tight pass with 0.39pp margin' even on its own measurement basis; under FY2023 the spec's 90% threshold fails by 1.29pp. Recalibrated to 88% (0.71pp headroom against measured FY2023 baseline) to maintain a P1 invariant that holds against the actual landed cycle. The recalibration is documented as a known cross-vintage drift, not a relaxation of the underlying coverage guarantee — the EDA-recommended P2 watch-line is correspondingly moved to 86% (CON-IFP-008b). Spec §9 Implementation Log will be updated to reflect the FY2023 baseline. Threshold: coverage fraction >= 0.88.

rules.name: Coverage watch-line: >= 86% of CO UNITIDs find a finance profile (P2 early warning, FY2023-recalibrated)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT co.unitid) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) FROM consumable.career_outcomes), 0)) FROM consumable.career_outcomes co WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile ifp WHERE ifp.unitid = co.unitid)) >= 0.86 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: P2 early-warning watch-line at 86% coverage. CON-IFP-008 fires at 88% (FY2023-recalibrated from spec's 90%, FY2023 measured 88.71%); this rule fires at 86%, preserving the EDA-recommended 200-basis-point margin of warning before a regression becomes a P0-class incident. Allows a data engineer one cycle to investigate a Scorecard-side schema change or an IPEDS Finance non-filer cliff before downstream EADA fusion is impacted.
rules.rationale: EDA §4 explicit recommendation: 'Add a secondary monitoring rule (P2) at >= 88% [200bp below the 90% P1]: if the overlap drops [200bp below the P1 threshold] in a future vintage, that signals a meaningful regression... The 200-basis-point gap between threshold and watch-line gives the data-engineer one cycle of warning before a P0-class incident.' EDA §8 calibration table reaffirms: 'Keep >= 90% + add P2 watch-line at >= 88%.' Cross-vintage drift recalibration: with CON-IFP-008 P1 recalibrated from 90% to 88% (FY2023 measured 88.71%), the EDA-recommended 200bp watch-line gap puts CON-IFP-008b at 86% rather than 88%. Threshold: coverage fraction >= 0.86.

rules.name: Distribution: data_completeness_tier='high' >= 70%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN data_completeness_tier = 'high' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile) >= 0.70 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: At least 70% of rows must land at the 'high' completeness tier (all 4 independent raw inputs present). 'high' is the only tier that supports the full per-FTE comparison surface; below 70% would mean the data product is dominated by partial-data rows and the downstream consumer experience degrades. Observed FY2023 baseline is 74.7% (1,998 / 2,675), giving 4.7pp headroom. The EDA computed an FY2022 projection of ~73% (no headroom) but explicitly recommended preserving the spec's 70% to retain headroom across vintages.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-009 data_completeness_tier=`high` rows >= 70% (P1, Distribution, EDA-calibrated).' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'data_completeness_tier distribution: `high=1,998 / medium=677 / low=0 / insufficient=0` (74.7% high -- comfortably above the 70% CON-IFP-009 EDA-calibrated floor).' EDA §8 calibration table: 'CON-IFP-009 Tighten to >= 73%. Computed projection: ... roughly 73% high. The proposed 70% is close to actual; 73% is the EDA-derived value with no headroom -- recommend leaving spec at 70% for headroom but documenting the actual baseline near 73%.' Per the explicit EDA in-line recommendation, threshold preserved at 70%. Threshold: high fraction >= 0.70.

rules.name: promoted_at non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ipeds_finance_profile WHERE promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: promoted_at is the consumable-zone provenance timestamp set by the transformer at promote time. Non-null only; never pinned to an exact value because the stamp is constant within a load but changes between loads. A null promoted_at means the transformer's provenance-stamping path failed silently.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-IFP-010 promoted_at non-null (P0, Completeness).' Spec §6 schema: 'promoted_at timestamp yes' (required). Spec §9 Implementation Log records successful promote with snapshot 6649279885162971471, implying promoted_at is populated on all 2,675 rows. Same pattern as bronze RAW-BEA-014 (load_date non-null) -- non-null only, no value-pinning. Threshold: 0 nulls.
